{{Infobox location
| name = Elysia
| introduced = House of Blades
}}

''See also: [[Elysia Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Elysia, also known as the [[City of Light]], is a magnificent territory characterized by its golden and silver architecture, featuring soaring towers, gleaming amethysts, and monuments. The city is protected by massive, shining walls that stand at least fifteen paces high, with entrances consisting of soundless sliding gates or white stone tunnels illuminated by golden torches. Inside, the city contains districts identified by colored doors, such as the [[Rose District]], and streets paved with flawless white stone. The surrounding landscape includes a grassy plain filled with gold and white flowers. Functionally, Elysia serves to maintain order among the various [[Territories]]. It is a place governed by [[nine virtues]], with selflessness serving as a foundational concept for its people. The city possesses unique properties, such as gates that typically only open once per day and a sky that appears yellow. Elysia also provides a potent, golden energy that can be harnessed by certain individuals to manifest physical objects like swords or bolts of light, though drawing upon this power can be physically and mentally exhausting. The city is home to various inhabitants, including gold soldiers, gnomes, and creatures associated with different colors like violet and silver. Historically, the entity is linked to the concept of an [[Incarnation]], a figure associated with great power and significant past conflicts.}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Known as the [[City of Light]], Elysia serves as the tenth [[Territory]] and is situated beyond a Gate that opens only once daily during daylight hours. The city is characterized by soaring towers, gleaming rooftops, and walls of pale white stone and pure gold that feature swirling patterns and stand at least fifteen paces high. Under a bright, rich golden sky, the landscape consists of gold sections and silver-and-[[diamond]] parapets, creating a realm of gold and rainbows. While the city contains impressive virtues and powers, it is not an ideal location for long-distance traveling. [[Alin]] is able to wrench a bolt of light from the city and can call upon Elysia as an item for power.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">14</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Elysia]] is known as the [[City of Light]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4">''House of Blades'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Elysia]] is a city located beyond a Gate.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c7">''House of Blades'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Elysia]] is the tenth [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The [[Gate to Elysia]] can only be opened once a day and while the sun is in the sky.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Elysia]]'s walls are made of pale white stone and pure gold with swirling patterns.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The sky in [[Elysia]] is a bright, rich shade of gold.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Elysia]] has shining walls that stretch at least fifteen paces over a person's head.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c8">''House of Blades'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Elysia]] contains impressive powers and virtues.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Elysia]] has a gold section.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c8" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Elysia]] is a location with gleaming rooftops and soaring towers.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16">''House of Blades'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Elysia]] is not the best [[Territory]] for long-distance [[Traveling]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] can wrench a bolt of light from [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Elysia]] is an item [[Alin]] can call upon for power.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17">''House of Blades'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Elysia]] is a city of gold and rainbows under a golden sun with silver-and-[[diamond]] parapets.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c21">''House of Blades'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|As the tenth [[Territory]], Elysia serves as a [[City of Light]] that embodies the enlightened virtues of man and acts as a complement to every other [[Territory]]. Historically, this realm functioned as the guardian of the [[Incarnations]] over three hundred years ago. While its walls are capable of defending against a foreign [[Incarnation]], the [[Territory]] may sometimes lack defenses. [[Alin]] experiences Elysia as a sunny light and is able to reach through space into the location, which once served as a place for [[Grandmaster Naraka]] to receive healing. During his journey, [[Alin]] draws power from the realm and summons a green shield from its [[essence]].}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">11</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Elysia]] is the tenth [[Territory]] and represents the enlightened virtues of man.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The walls of [[Elysia]] can defend against a foreign [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Elysia]] is a sunny light that [[Alin]] feels over his shoulder.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] summons a green shield from [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Elysia]] is a location where [[Grandmaster Naraka]] was taken for healing.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Elysia]] is a [[Territory]] that may lack defenses.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c16">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] draws power from [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Elysia]] is a [[City of Light]] that contains counters and complements to each other [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Elysia]] was the guardian of the [[Incarnations]] more than three hundred years ago.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can reach through space into [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Elysia]] is the complement to each [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Elysia is an ancient, warm [[Territory]] composed of nine colorful districts, including a [[Gold District]], a [[Violet District]], and a small white section. This location is characterized by an eternal sunrise and is protected by an invisible barrier at its Gate, which is subject to specific opening rules. Represented by a symbol of a rising sun behind a downward-pointing winged [[sword]], the city is home to noble citizens and gold-armored, winged warriors. Although it once fought Ragnarus and stands as the only opponent capable of facing [[Zakareth]] and [[Cynara]], it faces a new threat from [[King]] [[Zakareth]], who intends to loot its treasures. As the [[King]] moves against the city, a battle occurs within its bounds, even as [[Incarnations]] seek to eliminate both [[Alin]] and Elysia. Ultimately, returning to this [[City of Light]] means becoming part of its fabric forever, as the [[essence]] of the [[Blue Light]] returns to Elysia upon its dissolution.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">24</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysia]] is the only opponent that can stand against [[Zakareth]] and [[Cynara]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2">''City of Light'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysia]] is an ancient city built in nine districts.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7">''City of Light'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysia]] features an eternal sunrise.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysia]] is a location protected by an invisible barrier at its Gate.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysia]] is a place with specific rules regarding the opening of gates.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Returning to the [[City of Light]] means becoming a part of its fabric and never being able to leave.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|There may be [[Incarnations]] besides [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The symbol of [[Elysia]] is a winged [[sword]] point-down in front of a rising sun.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11">''City of Light'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysia]] is a city with multiple districts and colors.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysia]] contains a [[Violet District]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysia]] contains a [[Gold District]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysia]] contains a white part which is the smallest.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysia]] once fought Ragnarus.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c12">''City of Light'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysia]] has noble citizens.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c12" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[essence]] of the [[Blue Light]] returns to [[Elysia]] when it dissolves.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c12" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysia]] is warm.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c15">''City of Light'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] intends to loot the treasures of [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysia]] is a location where the group may have to fight.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25">''City of Light'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[King]] is moving against [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26">''City of Light'', chapter 26</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysia]] is the [[King]]'s real goal.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Incarnations]]' primary objective is to eliminate [[Alin]] and [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysia]] has gold-armored, winged warriors.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A battle occurred in [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c28">''City of Light'', chapter 28</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysia]] is a [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c28" />}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|"Once I returned to the City of Light, he would become a part of its fabric, never to leave again."<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Locations]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate locations]]
[[Category:Settlements]]
